title: Airbnb: Swimming in Free Cash Flow
text: This earnings analysis examines Airbnb's Q1 2024 performance, highlighting several key achievements. Financial Performance: The company reported revenue of $2.14 billion (up 18% year-over-year), net income of $264 million (up 126%), and free cash flow of $1.9 billion with an 89% FCF margin. Nights and Experiences Booked grew by 9.5% YoY, driven by growth across all regions and a substantial increase in app downloads. Mobile bookings reached 54% of total nights booked, with U.S. app downloads surging 60%. Regional growth was particularly strong in Asia Pacific (up 21%) and Latin America (up 19%). Groups of five or more people showed consistent growth across quarters. As the Merchant of Record, Airbnb benefits from holding customer cash before payouts. Interest income nearly doubled to $293 million, leveraging higher Treasury rates. The company also introduced new Icons experiences and group travel features. Despite strong half-year projections ($4.85 billion, exceeding Wall Street's $4.80 billion estimate), the stock declined 8% post-earnings. The analyst argues investors overreacted to Q2 guidance without considering broader performance context.
